Makefile for building a C-based chess library and tests with GCC, supporting debug and release modes.

# Purpose
The `Makefile` is used to automate the build process for a Chess implementation in C. It defines the compiler as `gcc` and sets the compiler flags to include all warnings. If the `DEBUG` variable is set to 1, the `-g` flag is added for debugging; otherwise, `-g0` is used to omit debugging information. The file specifies source files located in `src/chesslib` and `src` directories, and it excludes `src/tests.o` from the main object files. It handles platform independence by setting executable names differently for Windows and other operating systems. The `Makefile` includes targets for building the chess library (`chesslib`), running tests (`tests`), and creating the `bin` directory. It also provides a `clean` target to remove compiled object files and the `bin` directory, and a `test` target to execute the test suite.
